title: hygrophila ciliata
intro: Hygrophila ciliata, also known as Blume's water willow or water hygrophila, is a tropical aquatic plant native to India, Sri Lanka, and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which help in treating conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Hygrophila ciliata include reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or used topically for skin issues and wounds.
